rationale: This structure is organized around the major Earth systems (lithosphere, hydrosphere, atmosphere, cryosphere) and the key integrative or problem-focused fields that cut across them. The solid Earth sciences are split into Geology, Geophysics, and Geochemistry, reflecting major disciplinary divisions. Similarly, the 'fluid' Earth is covered by Atmospheric Sciences, Oceanography, Hydrology, and Glaciology. Crucially, integrative fields like Climate Science, Environmental Science, and Sustainability Science are given their own categories, as they represent enormous and distinct bodies of research that synthesize information from the other domains. This count of 14 provides sufficient granularity without becoming overwhelming, allowing distinct research communities like Paleontology and Soil Science to have their own home.

subspecialties.name: Climate Science
subspecialties.tagline: Modeling and analyzing Earth's long-term weather patterns and future changes.
covers: The study of climate systems, variability, and the impacts of global warming.
General circulation models (GCMs), climate attribution, carbon cycle, sea-level rise.
explicitly_excludes: Reconstruction of past climates (→ Paleoclimatology)
Short-term weather and atmospheric dynamics (→ Atmospheric Sciences)

subspecialties.name: Environmental Science & Management
subspecialties.tagline: Assessing and mitigating human impacts on the environment.
covers: The study of pollution, remediation, conservation, and resource management.
Water quality, land degradation, waste management, environmental impact assessment.
explicitly_excludes: The specific chemistry of pollutants (→ Environmental Chemistry)
Broader socio-ecological systems (→ Sustainability Science)

subspecialties.name: Geology
subspecialties.tagline: The study of the solid Earth, its rocks, and their history.
covers: The composition, structure, and evolution of the Earth's crust.
Sedimentology, structural geology, volcanology, mineralogy, geomorphology.
explicitly_excludes: Earth's large-scale physical processes (→ Geophysics)
The fossil record of life (→ Paleontology)
The chemical composition of rocks (→ Geochemistry)

subspecialties.name: Oceanography
subspecialties.tagline: Exploring the physical, chemical, and biological aspects of the ocean.
covers: The study of marine systems, from currents to ecosystems.
Ocean circulation, marine chemistry, marine biology, coastal processes.
explicitly_excludes: Long-term ocean warming trends as part of the global system (→ Climate Science)
Marine fossil record (→ Paleontology)

subspecialties.name: Atmospheric Sciences
subspecialties.tagline: The science of weather, atmosphere composition, and air dynamics.
covers: The study of the Earth's atmosphere and its phenomena.
Meteorology, atmospheric chemistry, cloud physics, weather forecasting.
explicitly_excludes: Long-term climate patterns and change (→ Climate Science)
Chemistry of air pollutants (→ Environmental Chemistry)

subspecialties.name: Hydrology
subspecialties.tagline: The movement, distribution, and quality of water on Earth.
covers: The study of the water cycle and water resources on and below the land surface.
Groundwater flow, surface runoff, watershed management, hydrogeology.
explicitly_excludes: Glaciers and ice sheets (→ Glaciology)
Ocean water systems (→ Oceanography)

subspecialties.name: Geophysics
subspecialties.tagline: Applying physics to understand the Earth's interior, tectonics, and fields.
covers: The study of the physical properties and processes of the Earth.
Seismology, plate tectonics, geomagnetism, gravity fields, geodesy.
explicitly_excludes: Rock composition and history (→ Geology)
Earth's chemical processes (→ Geochemistry)

subspecialties.name: Sustainability Science
subspecialties.tagline: Integrating human, environmental, and economic systems for a viable future.
covers: An integrated, problem-focused field studying socio-ecological systems.
Renewable energy systems, life cycle assessment, urban sustainability, food-energy-water nexus.
explicitly_excludes: Specific pollution problems (→ Environmental Science & Management)
Purely economic or policy studies (→ Social Sciences)

subspecialties.name: Geochemistry
subspecialties.tagline: The chemistry of the Earth's crust, mantle, and natural systems.
covers: The study of the chemical composition and processes of geological materials.
Isotope geology, mineral chemistry, petrogenesis, planetary geochemistry.
explicitly_excludes: Pollutants in contemporary environments (→ Environmental Chemistry)
Bulk physical properties of the Earth (→ Geophysics)

subspecialties.name: Environmental Chemistry
subspecialties.tagline: The fate and effects of chemicals in air, water, and soil.
covers: The study of chemical species in the natural and polluted environment.
Contaminant transport, atmospheric aerosols, aquatic chemistry, soil pollution.
explicitly_excludes: Geological-scale chemical processes (→ Geochemistry)
Broad atmospheric physics and dynamics (→ Atmospheric Sciences)

subspecialties.name: Paleoclimatology
subspecialties.tagline: Reconstructing past climates using natural archives like ice cores and sediments.
covers: The study of climate history across geological time.
Proxy data analysis, ice cores, sediment cores, dendroclimatology, paleoceanography.
explicitly_excludes: Modern and future climate modeling (→ Climate Science)
Fossil analysis for evolutionary history (→ Paleontology)

subspecialties.name: Soil Science
subspecialties.tagline: The formation, classification, and management of soils.
covers: The study of soil as a natural resource and living system.
Pedology, soil physics, soil chemistry, soil biology, land management.
explicitly_excludes: Broader watershed systems (→ Hydrology)
Bedrock geology (→ Geology)

subspecialties.name: Paleontology
subspecialties.tagline: The study of ancient life through the fossil record.
covers: The study of the history of life on Earth based on fossils.
Vertebrate and invertebrate paleontology, paleobotany, micropaleontology, paleoecology.
explicitly_excludes: Study of living organisms and ecosystems (→ Ecology & Evolution)
Using microfossils purely as climate proxies (→ Paleoclimatology)

subspecialties.name: Glaciology
subspecialties.tagline: The science of glaciers, ice sheets, and other forms of ice.
covers: The study of ice and its physical properties in the environment.
Ice sheet dynamics, glacier mass balance, sea ice, permafrost.
explicitly_excludes: Water in its liquid state (→ Hydrology)
Using ice cores primarily to reconstruct past climates (→ Paleoclimatology)

uncovered_concerns: The high degree of interdisciplinarity means some papers will be difficult to classify; for example, 'ocean acidification' could fit Oceanography, Climate Science, or Environmental Chemistry depending on its focus.
Physical Geography was not included as a category, as its core subfields are represented here (e.g., geomorphology in Geology, plus Hydrology, Glaciology, and Climate Science).
Planetary Science is often housed in Earth Science departments but was not given its own category; relevant papers would likely fall under Geophysics or Geochemistry.
Methodological fields like Remote Sensing & GIS were excluded, as they are tools applied across all other subspecialties rather than distinct research domains.
